content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5669           322-3901            322-4441            322-6444

February 5, 1981

Jim Alford
1905 Elm
McKinleyville, CA 95521

Dear Mr. Alford:

In your letter of January 21, 1981, you requested advice
concerning a potential conflict of interest. The facts, as
I understand them, are as follows.

You have been a Humboldt County planning commissioner
since July 1979. From May through August 1979 you were
employed as a fish buyer at an hourly wage by Eureka Fisheries.
As a planning commissioner you are called upon to make deci-
sions on local coastal planning matters. You have asked us
to advise you as to

1. Whether you should have disqualified yourself
from decisions on coastal matters in August 1979.

2. Whether you should disqualify yourself at this
time on coastal matters.

As to question one, you have not provided enough infor-
mation for me to advise you on the propriety of your past
actions. Generally, this Commission does not give advice as
to such actions. Rather, we prefer to render advice which
can guide future actions.

The answer to the second question always depends upon
the particular facts of a particular case. However, from
the facts you have given there appears to be no grounds at
all for your disqualification from the decision making process
because of your past employment as a fish buyer.

Jim Alford
February 5, 1981
Page Two


     Government Code Sections 87100-87103* forbid a public
official from making or influencing a governmental decision
in which he has a financial interest. Section 87103 defines
"financial interest" in part as

     (c) Any source of income ... aggregating ...
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within twelve months
prior to the time when the decision is made....

     Thus, assuming that your income from Eureka Fisheries
totaled $250 or more, you would have to disqualify yourself
from making decisions which could have a material financial
effect, on Eureka Fisheries only, within 12 months of working
there. Since you have not been employed by Eureka Fisheries
for over a year, there appears to be no reason that you
cannot vote on any matter concerning Eureka Fisheries.

     I hope this has been helpful to you. Please contact me
if you have further questions.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [Signature]
                                        Stella Connell Levy
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

SCL:kp


*All statutory references are to the Government Code.

Jan 21 January 1981
1905 Elm
McKinleyville CA 95521

Sandra Cameron
Fair Political Practices Commission
1600 K. St. Mall
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Ms. Cameron,

I understand your office can determine if a conflict of interest does indeed exist for an office holder. I am a Humboldt County Planning Commissioner. I was appointed in July 1979. From May to late August 1979 I was employed by Eureka Fisheries as a fish buyer at an hourly wage. I have held no management or ownership positions in the fishing industry during my term.

Our county counsel has indicated I may have a conflict of interest on local coastal planning matters since I was for nearly two months an employee in the fishing industry. According to my agency's code (adopted by the Board of Supervisors June 1980) there are four reasons listed in Section 8a-d for disqualification.

Please tell me if there were grounds for my disqualification in August 1979. Please tell me if I was liable under the Code adopted ten months later.

Thank you,

[signature]
Jim Alford

JTA/bjm
